series_title: Ohio Health Plan Program Correspondence and Vendor Documentation
series_description: Files contain correspondence related to the programs administered by Ohio Health Plans and may include inquiries and responses with the federal government, hospitals, stakeholders and interest groups regarding program operations. Files may also include copies of requests for proposals, vendor selection justification and evaluation criteria, and progress reports as backup documentation. All active records identified as being needed by the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) for their daily operations as of July 1, 2013, were transferred by the Ohio Department of Job and Family Services (ODJFS) to ODM. Any records that are inactive and identified as being not necessary for daily operations by ODM were retained by ODJFS.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years and 3 years in records center ; Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value

series_description: Documents for retention: Surs Overview, Plan and Status Update Reports, Final Advance Med/ODJFS Med/Medi JOA and Information, Project Advance Med, UAT HIPAA-Phase 3, Project Plans, Changes, Issues, Phase Evaluations, Risk, Status Reports, Net Effect, MedStat Deliverable, Provider Master File Enhancements, Med Stat (FADS), DSS Exec Summary, DSS RFP Session, MedStat Training Guides, MedStat Budget, Med Stat APD, User GRP Mtgs, Survey, Profile, Phase 5, RFP Manuals/Docs/SAS Manuals.All active records identified as being needed by the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) for their daily operations as of July 1, 2013, were transferred by the Ohio Department of Job and Family Services (ODJFS) to ODM. Any records that are inactive and identified as being not necessary for daily operations by ODM were retained by ODJFS.
retention_statement: Paper: In office and/ or in storage 3years

series_description: Long term care reconciliation project documentation for nursing home credit balances not included in the Combined Proposed Adjudication Order (final fiscal audits) which was completed in 2007. Documentation includes, but is not limited to, spreadsheets with providers and dollar amounts, certification numbers for mailing, response letters, results, and statistics.All active records identified as being needed by the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) for their daily operations as of July 1, 2013, were transferred by the Ohio Department of Job and Family Services (ODJFS) to ODM. Any records that are inactive and identified as being not necessary for daily operations by ODM were retained by ODJFS.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 7 years in the records center

series_description: Long Term Care Facility Verification Documentation for Case Mix, Franchise Fee, and Rate Tables Bureau/section toll statements and logs of telephone calls with provider, attorneys, accountants, consultants, and other involved in the health care field to conduct the business of Long Term Care (LTC) facilities.
retention_statement: Paper: P/E Retain 3 year in office

series_description: Used to review cases for authorizing treatment and or service for Medicaid recipients. Requests may be either approved or denied. Types of treatment and/or service include: Supplies; ambulance; ambulate; dental; durable medical equipment, occupational therapy, out-patient hospital visits, pharmaceuticals-drugs, food supplements, physician visits, private nurse duty, hospital rehabilitation, special committee cases, transplant, and vision care. Includes: forms ODHS 3142, ODHS 3612, and ODHS 3542. Hard copy prior authorization requests are verified against MMIS requests listing.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until MMIS data entry is complete. Transfer to Records Automation to be digitally scanned to compact disc. Destroy hard copy 60(sixty) days after quality review is completed. Destroy hard copy 60 days after quality review. ; Electronic: Retain master and duplicate CD’s for 7 years

series_description: Cost allocation plans and related correspondence to and from the federalgovernment detailing operating expenditures of the agency.All active records identified as being needed by the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) for their daily operations as of July 1, 2013, were transferred by the Ohio Department of Job and Family Services (ODJFS) to ODM. Any records that are inactive and identified as being not necessary for daily operations by ODM were retained by ODJFS.
retention_statement: Microform: Retain 3 years in office ; Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value, and ; Paper: Retain in office 3 years and 4 years in the records center
disposition: Microform: Shred ; Electronic: Erase ; Paper: Shred

series_description: Record includes worksheets, statistical information and rational forallocating administrative costs for the agency.All active records identified as being needed by the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) for their daily operations as of July 1, 2013, were transferred by the Ohio Department of Job and Family Services (ODJFS) to ODM. Any records that are inactive and identified as being not necessary for daily operations by ODM were retained by ODJFS.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 3 years and 4 years in the records center ; Paper: Retain in office 3 years and 4 years in the records center

series_description: Delinquent accounts that have been certified to the Attorney General office to collect on outstanding monies and or overpayments for goods or services rendered.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 years

series_description: Record documents the Agency’s reconciliation of expenditures in the Ohio Administrative Knowledge System (OAKS, Ohio’s computer system) to ODJFS federal expenditure reports to ensure the department maximizes federal reimbursements.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 3 years and 4 years in the records center ; Electronic: Retain in office 3 years and 4 years in the records center

series_description: Used to review cases for authorizing treatment and or service for Medicaid recipients. Requests may be either approved or denied Types of treatment and/or service include: supplies; ambulance; ambulate; dental; durable medical equipment; occupational therapy; out-patient hospital visit; pharmaceuticals-drugs; food supplements; physician visits; private duty nurses; rehabilitation hospital; special committee cases; transplant; and vision care. Includes: ODHS 3142; ODSH 3612; and ODHS 3542.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until MMIS data entry is complete. Transfer to Records Conversion to be digitally scanned to compact disc. Destroy hard copy 60 days after quality review. ; Electronic: Retain master and duplicate CD''''s for 7 years

series_description: Posts made or approved by the Office of Communications on Social Media sites such as Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn etc. are made to market the agency and share information that is of interest to the citizens of Ohio. This information can include but not be limited to; press releases, photos, videos, events, and general information about the agency and the services we provide. These web pages are maintained by the owners of the sites and not ODM. All active records identified as being needed by the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) for their daily operations as of July 1, 2013, were transferred by the Ohio Department of Job and Family Services (ODJFS) to ODM. Any records that are inactive and identified as being not necessary for daily operations by ODM were retained by ODJFS.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for two months from date posted

series_description: Working papers and electronic documents received by ODM program areas in response to a media request for information. These records are used by the department’s public information officers for the development of the department’s official response to the media on issues related to agency programs, services or administration. All active records identified as being needed by the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) for their daily operations as of July 1, 2013, were transferred by the Ohio Department of Job and Family Services (ODJFS) to ODM. Any records that are inactive and identified as being not necessary for daily operations by ODM were retained by ODJFS
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value, and ; Paper: Retain until no longer of administrative value, and

series_description: Materials used in developing agency pamphlet publications, which are organized by pamphlet title. All active records identified as being needed by the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) for their daily operations as of July 1, 2013, were transferred by the Ohio Department of Job and Family Services (ODJFS) to ODM. Any records that are inactive and identified as being not necessary for daily operations by ODM were retained by ODJFS
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until pamphlet is no longer created ; Paper: Retain until pamphlet is no longer created

series_title: Ohio Medicaid Consumer Hotline Call Recording
series_description: All records of recordings of telephone calls received by the Ohio Medicaid Consumer Hotline identified as being needed by the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 1 year

series_title: Medicaid Interim/Final Settlement & Rate Setting Packages
series_description: Interim/final settlements and rate setting packages of services provided by Medicaid providers, ex. Medicaid School Program (MSP), hospitals, PASSPORTS, public developmental centers, lead investigation, clinics. File contains but is not limited to cost report forms, settlement and rate setting work papers, settlement spreadsheets and other documentation supporting the interim/final settlements and rate setting/rate adjustments.
retention_statement: Paper: In office and /or in storage, retain for 7 years from adjudication date or date used for rate setting purposes ; Electronic: On department computers or department''s shared drives, retain for 7 years from adjudication date or date used for rate setting purposes

series_description: Files contain notes, reports and draft correspondence used for developing health plan social program legislation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years and 3 years in the records center ; Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value, review by State and

series_description: Reports written by enforcement officers when monitoring ODJFS and the Department of Medicaid offices in the counties to ensure compliance with EEO guidelines. The reports include the number of clients served, staff and facility assessments, reviews of job applications and orders, comments and response to the reports.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years ; Electronic: Retain in office 7 years

series_description: Civil Rights plans documenting compliance with affirmative action and EEO requirements for 88 counties and 44+ Children's Service Boards. Also, includes county Child Protection and Oversight Reports showing compliance status.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years, Review by State prior to disposition. ; Electronic: Retain in office 7 years. Review by State Archives prior to disposition.

series_title: Third Party Insurance and Accident Recovery Files
series_description: Case files containing legal and claims documentation from Medicaid recipients involved in health insurance and accident (TORT)suits involving another party to recover eligible benefits. Files include case and claim numbers, recipient personal data and medical history, provider and benefit information, copies of benefit warrants, and benefit amounts recouped as result of settlements. Unresolved cases are referred to the Attorney General’s Office for disposition.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 30 days after hard copy is imaged and quality review is complete, and ; Electronic: Retain in office 1 year after case is closed and 6 years in the records center

series_description: Closed/settled Third Party Recovery cases handled by contractors. May contain Medical Histories, Authorization to gain medical history, signed approval documents (06613), and Unit correspondence, letters of settlement, copies of issued warrants, worksheets, and computer printouts of the recipient history profile.
retention_statement: Paper: All paper forms are now scanned or received in digital format. Retain 30 days after hard copy is imaged and quality review is complete, and ; Electronic: Retain in office for 1 year.

series_description: Proposed changes and additions to Social Services Programs, submitted to federal government for approval. Includes documentation on reviews and approvals by state officials, program descriptions, fiscal information, and legal citations.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 15 years, or until superseded; ; Electronic: Retain 15 years, or until superseded;

series_description: Employee working files that reside on their computer or shares commonly known as P:drive, OneDrive, etc.
retention_statement: Electronic: Files are to be kept until such time that the official file is placed in an appropriate record storage area or the employee has been separated for 30 days.

series_description: Record contains employment history, payroll earnings, and deduction information. May contain Personnel Action, Withholding Certificates, Payroll Deduction, Deferred Compensation, Health Care, Dental Care, Disability Application, and Request for Leave, Address Change, Payroll Record, and W2 Roster.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain electronically in Onbase for 7 years

series_description: Requests for compensation due to accident on the job. May contain copies of accident reports, request forms and 3rd party information to substantiate the claim.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years ; Electronic: Retain 7 years and

series_description: Job audits provide information regarding job duties for a position. Files may contain State of Ohio job audit questionnaire, filing letter, position description, and a copy of the DAS final report.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years and ; Electronic: Retain in office 7 years and

series_title: Independent Medical Exam and Disability Separation Files
series_description: Independent medical examinations are requested for employees for a variety of medical conditions to determine if they are fit for duty. If the employee is determined to be unfit for work, involuntary disability separation paperwork is filed. Files include personal medical history, requests for leave, physician reports, board order, letter directing the employee to report to work or disability determination letter and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years and ; Electronic: Retain in office 7 years and

series_description: Various project studies conducted on position specific classification placements, promotions, salaries, and lay-offs. Data used to analyze trends and improve HR operations and assistance to employees.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 7 years and ; Electronic: Retain in office for 7 years and

series_description: Hiring materials and job bid files for Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) positions. May contain vacancy postings, personnel requisitions, position description, applications (Hard copy and electronic), resumes, test questions, and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 6 months after decision is made to fill or not to fill a position, transfer to records center for 7 years ; Electronic: Retain in office 6 months after decision is made to fill or not to fill a position, transfer to records center for 7 years

series_description: Structured interview, assessment and selection documentation for Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) positions. Files may contain proficiency tests, oral and written interviews, various hands-on computer exercises and scoring information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office until updated ; Paper: Retain in office until updated

series_description: Employee requests for coverage under the Family Medical Leave Act for a variety of short term and chronic medical conditions. Files include personal medical history, requests for leave, physician statements, determination letters and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years and ; Electronic: Retain in office 7 years and

series_description: A detailed description of job duties, knowledge, skills and abilities for each position. May contain original signed position description; PSMQ (position specific minimum qualifications), requests to DAS for PSMQ and DAS approval.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until updated and ; Electronic: Retain in office until updated and

series_description: Alphabetically organized case files documenting decisions on disciplinary issues, whether or not discipline actually ensues. These records substantiate "just cause" for discipline. File may include the request for discipline, copies of prior disciplinary action, investigatory reports and statements, copies arbitration decisions and settlement agreements, and Labor Relations recommendations. Case files are referenced in instances where progressive discipline is being considered.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years. ; Electronic: Retain in office 7 years.

series_title: Employee Disputes, Litigation and Performance Review Appeals
series_description: Personnel disputes and appeals on a variety of work related performance and labor issues filed before the State Personnel Board of Review and the State Employee Review Board. Files include supporting documentation related to the issue, investigatory reports and statements, and the rendered decision.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years and ; Electronic: Retain in office 7 years and

series_title: Reorganization, Layoff, and Job Abolishment Files
series_description: Contains documentation supporting layoff, job abolishment, and/or reorganizations within ODM. Documentation may consist of initial proposals, justification for proposed action(s); approval correspondence from other entities as required (Office of Collective Bargaining).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years and ; Electronic: Retain in office 7 years and

series_description: Record of employee training, course offerings, and career development opportunities. Files include electronic applications, copies of transcripts, rosters, agendas, curricula, course evaluation and training certificates. Applications document individual training record used to register participants, listing each class attendee by name, working title, department, course title, hours and date of training session, and may include race, sex, and physically challenged entries which are voluntary. Course offerings include, but are not limited to, individual skill development, program operations, QSTP, computer systems and software, and management/supervisory development. Data is tracked throughout the employee’s tenure with the department.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years after employee terminates service with the department and ; Electronic: Retain in office 7 years after employee terminates service with the department and

series_title: Managed Care Plan & Enhanced Care Management
series_description: Record contains specific files related to each Managed Care plan (MCP) and the monitoring of services provided to Medicaid recipients as part of the plan. may include MCP program operating and reimbursement documents, Request For Proposals(RFP), listings of providers involved in the MCP; enrollment eligibility information; rate setting documents; wavier request; quality assurance audit reviews; financial reports; capitation reports; historical data; grievance information (SACMS evaluations), encounter data and Just Cause requests from recipients to change MCPs.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years and 5 years in the records center ; Electronic: Retain in office 2 years and 5 years in the records center

series_description: The Bureau of Program Integrity (BPI) may either conduct an audit or monitor and review audits performed on behalf of the department. The purpose of all Medicaid audits to ensure payments made were reasonable and necessary, and that applicable laws, regulations and contractual agreements were followed.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until audit has been released ; Electronic: Retain in office until audit has been released

series_description: Monthly sample reviews, e.g., nursing homes, disabled and aged persons, conducted by quality assessment staff to evaluate recipient eligibility and level of care under the Medicaid program and ensure county offices are providing services in compliance with program and agency operations, procedures, policy and directives as well as state and federal mandates. Files include, but are not limited to, client face sheets which contain information such as name, address, case number, service date, and eligibility determination data, worksheets, review schedules and CPAS (Medicaid claims processing assessment system). Data obtained from these reviews may be used to calculate program error rates and to identify problem areas that would likely benefit from corrective action. Files also include reports of findings and recommendations to improve processes.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 7 years

series_description: This record contains documentation of civil reviews by SUR analysts investigating Medicaid providers (e.g., doctors, dentists, medical suppliers, pharmacies and ambulance services) for potential over/under utilization of services. Reviews are conducted on providers from random samples of their records which must support the type of service and charges being billed. File contains, but is not limited to, records of procedures taken by analysts during an investigation; settlement agreements and adjudication orders; related correspondence, forms, logs, random samples used to identify and build cases on potential abusers; recipient complaints against providers; remittance statements; 1099 listing; and SUR subsystem reports.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until case audits are settled and closed and 5 years in the records center. ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced

series_description: Records of the Hospital Utilization Review Contractor is decisions concerning hospital pre-admission screening of Medicaid requests for inpatient services. Surveillance Utilization Review (SUR) staff oversees the reviews to ensure the Hospital Utilization Review Contractor is performing their functions as contracted. File includes, but are not limited to, documentation indicating what the review is for, approval, dollar amounts, reason why requests were denied, number of monthly denials by hospital, and appeals.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced ; Paper: Retain 5 years, and

series_description: Audit packs that are closed for settlement on a particular year. Packs contain 13 legal size folders scheduled as follows and containing the audit work papers. 1. Cost Report, 2. General, 3. Exit, 4. Assets, 5. Property, 6. Liabilities, 7. Equity, 8. Personal Allowance, 9. Statistics, 10. Revenue, 11. Expenses, 12. Payroll, and 13. Cash Disbursement.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area until adjudication ; Electronic: Retain Machine Readable copies in office for 7 years after the date of adjudication. Destroy after review by the Chief of Long-term Care

series_title: Medicaid Health Insurance Portability and Accountability ACT (HIPAA) Project Files
series_description: Project files document the development, planning and implementation of policies and procedures of the committees and subcommittees administering HIPAA. Files include, but are not limited to, background documentation, committee and subcommittee titles, workgroup titles, names of team members, agendas, meeting minutes, decisions made regarding the project, reports and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years. Review by State prior to disposal. ; Paper: Retain in office 5 years. Review by State Archives prior to disposal.

series_description: Documents collected for use as evidence in disputes between employees/terminated and ODM.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in Records Center for 5 years ; Electronic: Retain in Records Center for 5 years

series_description: Files include court docket papers, correspondence, and legal research for litigation involving ODM.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until scanned and ; Electronic: Retain 2 years after case is closed and no further appeals can be filed

series_description: Card file summarizing 119 Hearings include case name, court, docket number, case synopsis; and list of filed pleadings, judgments used to compile annual reports.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned into electronic format ; Electronic: Retain 2 years after case is closed and no further appeals can be filed, and

series_description: Rules, regulations, bylaws, and/or standards of agency which may be codified and made part of the Ohio Administrative Code. Files may contain letters proposing rules, letters making rules effective, public hearing notices, rules summaries and fiscal analysis.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 15 years after sent to Records Center. Records to be reviewed by State and ; Paper: Retain 15 years after sent to Records Center. Records to be reviewed by State Archives and

series_description: These are provider appeals to audit findings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in operating area 1 year, 15 years in Records Center and

series_description: Requests from the public for ODM records. (Confidential information is redacted).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years from date of request being fulfilled. ; Electronic: Retain 3 years from date of request being fulfilled.

series_title: Electronic Rule Filing Documents for other Agencies
series_description: Documents required in the rule-filing process that the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) enters electronically into the systems of other state agencies or entities including documents entered into any electronic rule-filing system of the Legislative Service Commission (LSC) such as Electronic Rule Filing (ERF).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until all data has been entered into the system of the other agencies and ; Electronic: Retain until all data has been entered into the system of the other agencies and

series_description: ODM forms, “Authorization for Clearance, Rules Filing, and Publication,” signed by attorneys to authorize the filing of rules and the issuance of transmittal letters by the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM). Signed transmittal letters without attachments used by ODM to notify stakeholder groups and ODM / ODJFS County staff of proposed and final filed rules; Testimony of interested parties at public hearings held on proposed rules by ODM, including audiotapes and transcripts when created; Requests for waivers of clearance in the rulemaking process; Notices of proposed rules by ODM that are mandated by Federal or State law but not filed in the Electronic Rule Filing System (ERF) that are sent to the Register of Ohio (ROO) and not published in newspapers; Comments submitted by interested members of the public by means other than the clearance electronic system of record in response to draft rules and other draft documents in clearance.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one year in office ; Electronic: Retain audio cassettes in office for one year

series_description: Comments submitted to the electronic system of record by interested members of the public, ODM, and ODJFs county staff in response to draft rules and other draft documents posted to the clearance electronic system of record. Responses by ODM staff to comments submitted to the electronic system of record by interested members of the public, ODM, and ODJFS county staff, in response to draft rules and other draft documents posted to the clearance electronic system of record.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 5 years or until the database is superseded by a new electronic system and

series_description: Directories, correspondence and reports related to agency participation in the Minority Business Enterprise Program. Records detail goods and services transactions with minority business vendors.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 years ; Electronic: Retain in office 5 years

series_description: Requests for proposal (RFP) and contracts with medical providers, e.g. physicians, dentists, hospitals, ambulatory services, medical technical advisors and medical suppliers for services under the Medicaid program. Files include letterhead bids, vendor and evaluation criteria, contractor evaluation, vendor selection justification, copies of purchase orders and invoices, contractor reports and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years in office, 3 years in Records Center ; Electronic: Retain 5 years in office

series_description: Alphabetical files by vendor of agency purchase orders to include requisitions and supporting documentation of these requests, e.g. bids, signature approvals, pricing and copies of invoices.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 1 year, until audited by the Auditor of State and audited report is released and discrepancies are resolved ; Electronic: Retain in office for 1 year, until audited by the Auditor of State and audited report is released and discrepancies are resolved

series_title: Contract / Grant Agreements and Related Documentation
series_description: ODM contracts, grant agreements, inter-agency agreements, industry standard and miscellaneous agreements and DAS state terms schedules for goods and services. Files include the Request for Agreement, Request for Amendment to Agreement, the actual agreements, Approval and Justification sheet, Request for release and Permit for Controlling Board approval, budget justification, Status Tracking Sheet, and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 8 years after the agreement expires. Destroy provided state and federal audits are released and all discrepancies are resolved ; Electronic: Retain 8 years after the agreement expires. Destroy provided state and federal audits are released and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: The EVV team generates various documents in support of program goals and agency initiatives. Records may include general correspondence, management and operations reports, manuals and handbooks, system specifications, technical documents, and data reports. The records generated may include individual private Protected Health Information (PHI) or Confidential Personal Information (CPI). The EVV System receives visit data from providers for certain home and community-based services. This information contains information such as date, time, type of service, direct care worker, billing provider, individual Medicaid identification, address and phone numbers for individuals and providers, location of visit, and exception codes. This visit data is used in the adjudication of claims. All records are used in support of EVV agency activities.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 10 years ; Electronic: Retain on agency drive for 10 years

series_description: This record contains documentation of medical/mental disability determination eligibility decisions. File includes, but is not limited to, decisions for approvals, denials, or referrals for Medicaid and Disability Assistance Programs. Form number (JFS) 3600 documents the decision. The Client Registry Information System-Enhanced (CRIS-E) tracks the determination decisions and informs the originating county of the case status.
retention_statement: Microform: Retain master and duplicate film 7 years ; Paper: Retain 60 days after hard copy is prepped, scanned and indexed (imaged) and quality control iscompleted, and ; Electronic: Retain in MIS on optical disk for 7 years
disposition: Microform: Shred ; Paper: Shred ; Electronic: Erase

series_description: The Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) provides optional Medicaid coverage to certain women in need of treatment for breast or cervical cancer, including pre-cancerous conditions in accordance with the Breast and Cervical Cancer Prevention and Treatment Act of 2000. ODM is responsible for retaining eligibility applications and treatment plans until case concludes , and then destruction after one (1) year.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office until case concludes;

series_description: Claims are received from physicians, dentists, medical suppliers and various providers for payment of services rendered to Medicaid recipients. Records contain detailed information regarding recipient’s background, medical history, provider, type of service, and complete cost breakouts of services rendered to ensure compliance with CMS requirements and Medicaid program policy to minimize potential billing discrepancies. Claims are submitted into the MITS system via electronic imaging, portal or EDI applications. Prior to July, 2011 claims were keyed into the system manually either by internal staff or by a contracted vendor.
retention_statement: Microform: Retain 90 days after claim is imaged and quality review is complete, and ; Paper: Retain 90 days after claim is imaged and quality review is complete, and ; Electronic: Retain digital image for 7 years after hard copy is imaged and
disposition: Microform: Shred ; Paper: Shred ; Electronic: Erase

series_description: Two different notices were mailed out for claims closure and returned as undeliverable. The first notice stated that even though individual client Medicaid cases were closed, medical bill were still being paid through error. There was no need for repayment, but if the client wanted to continue to be eligible for Medicaid, he or she needed to contact the County Dept. of Job and Family Services by 5/4/01. The second prior notice was sent to the affected individuals stating that our records show that he or she had not applied for continuing Medicaid assistance and proposed to stop Medicaid on 7/13/01.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 1 year. Retain in approved Records Center for 4 years, and

series_description: This record documents adjustments made to invoices from Medicaid providers for services rendered. Files contain adjustment request forms (ODJFS 6766 and 6767) and related correspondence detailing payment discrepancies or erroneous payments to providers. This documentation may be used by other ODJFS sections, the Attorney General’s office, or federal auditors to determine the correctness of payments, compliance to policy requirements, federal financial participation, and as supporting information in fraud investigations.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 90 days after hard copy is imaged and ; Microform: Retain 90 days after hard copy is imaged and ; Electronic: Retain 90 days after hard copy is imaged and
disposition: Paper: Shred ; Microform: Shred ; Electronic: Erase

series_description: Unprocessed ODJFS 6766/6767 Adjustment Request for unprocessed provider refund checks and corresponding documentation prior to the implementation of the Medicaid Information Technology System (MITS) on August 2, 2011. Information contained in these documents remained unprocessed at the closure of Medicaid Management Information System (MMIS). New business processes precluded them from being entered in MITS at "Go Live". Check information spans 7/2008 through 12/2010.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 90 days after hard copy is imaged and ; Electronic: Retain digital image for 7 years after hard copy is imaged and

series_description: Payment or recoupment files that contain records of Medicaid provider’s payments made to or due the state. Include, but not limited to collection reports and records, detailed suspense reports, credit balance reports and lien reports.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 7 years ; Electronic: Retain for 7 years

series_description: Operational logs and reports documenting the processing of Medicaid claims for payment. Record includes the claim type, transaction control #, claims status updates, suspense file analysis, date spans of the reels of microfilm, camera operator and date of filming.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced ; Paper: Retain in office 1 year

series_description: Ad hoc reports generated by MIS from the MMIS, Medicaid Management Information System, including MMIS sub-systems Control D and MARS, Monthly Administrative Reporting System to monitor payments to providers for services provided to Medicaid recipients to ensure services and claims are being paid/denied in accordance with Ohio Medicaid program requirements. Data includes medical health care eligibility, health care insurance, prior authorization, surveillance and utilization audit findings, case and claims/remittance information and buy-in summaries/totals of premiums paid by providers and recipients for health care services. Information is used to monitor operational performance and cross-match state and county welfare records to minimize duplicate payments.
retention_statement: Microform: Retain systems generated microfiche 7 years, and ; Electronic: Retain 7 years and ; Paper: Printed reports in office 1 year
disposition: Microform: Shred ; Electronic: Erase ; Paper: Shred

series_title: Medicaid Check Adjustments (Fee for Service and Long Term Care Providers)
series_description: Record contains history only adjustments as the result of the provider reimbursing Medicaid by a check beginning with a processing date of July 1, 2007 or after. It includes ODJFS adjustment request forms 6766 and 6767, and nursing facility payment and adjustment authorization forms 9400, check deposit records, copies of checks, and letters documenting overpayments. It may also include documents sent to and from the Attorney General’s Office, state and federal auditors or Surveillance and Utilization Review relative to the overpayment.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 6 months ; Microform: Retain master and duplicate microfilm for 7 years ; Electronic: Retain master and duplicate images on compact disc for 7 years
disposition: Paper: Shred ; Microform: Shred ; Electronic: Erase

series_title: Medicaid Management Information System (MMIS) Reference File Sub-System
series_description: Forms (JFS 06660,06662,06663,6679,6680,6681,6682,and 6683) and supporting documentation used to make changes and/or addition to the reference file sub-system of the Medicaid Management Information System(MMIS) for procedure, drug, diagnosis, and provider charge file fees under the Medicaid Program. File contain the required approval signatures for the change request, date of approval, and date the documentation was received and entered into the on-line system. The files are reference repeatedly as Medicaid invoices are processed to determine the quantity and or amount of payment to providers. System reports include procedure, drug, diagnosis codes, and provider charge file codes.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced ; Paper: Retain in office 3 years or until audit reviews are completed, reports released and all discrepancies are resolved, and

series_description: Provider applications and registration of those physicians, pharmacists, dentists, ambulatory services, medical suppliers, and nonprofit organizations which provide services to Medicaid recipients. Files may contain additional information reflecting changes in provider rates, staffing, services, ownership, or organizations which may affect the provider’s status. Organized by provider number, the file may include, but is not limited to, license and certification credentials; any or all of the following application, change and verification forms T125, ODHS 6750, 6751, 6752 and 6777; and copies of MMIS screen data listing the provider name and address, license, application date, recertification date, service codes and data entry operator identifier.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 60 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), indexed, and quality control is complete ; Electronic: Machine Readable: Retain until audited by the Auditor of State, audit report is released and discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: Internal production reports and system generated reports from the Medicaid Management Information System (MMIS) that detail provider activity, the number of providers, the number of applications and enrollment registrations, provider assistance call summaries, number of claims reviewed, and the ombudsman training and meeting assignments that are held with provider groups.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years and 3 years in the records center ; Electronic: Retain in office 2 years and 3 years in the records center

series_description: Contracts between the department and a provider of services for waiver programs. Authorized services vary by waiver program including adult day care, medical supplies, personal care, social work/ counseling, home-delivered meals, homemaker services resp.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until contract is canceled. Store in State Records Center for 7 years, and ; Electronic: Retain in office until contract is canceled. Store in State Records Center for 7 years, and

series_description: Provider application and personal data, Notices of Operational Deficiency(NODS), certified non-compliance notifications, Proposed Adjudication Orders(PAOs), termination, BCI/FBI background checks.
retention_statement: Paper: P/E Retain 3 years in office, 4 years in records center

series_title: Money Follows the Person (MFP) and Program Contract Documentation
series_description: MFP HOME Choice and Ohio Access Success records of BHCS home and community-based services programs and waivers, including but not limited to: Request for Proposal and related documentation,ODJFS contract and related documentation, contract amendments, quality management plans and reports, contract deliverable reports and responses, contracts invoices, contracts expenditures, task tracking documents, and quarterly and semi-annual reporting documentations to CMS.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 4 years until receipt of final payment or until any audit findings or lawsuits Have been resolved. ; Paper: Retain in office 60 days after imaging and destroy.

series_title: Money Follows the Person (MFP) Policy, Training, and Development
series_description: Rules, policy and program development files for the Money Follows the Person (MFP) Project which provides home and community based alternatives to institutional long term care. Includes policy drafts,Amendments, copies of published rules, policy applications, monitoring reports and related correspondence. Training documentations for case management contractors, HOME Choice- MFP Project and County Departments of Job & Family Services. Files include by are not limited to agendas, roster, handouts, training development notes and manuals, presentations and evaluations of training on Consumer case management, new forms, care of consumers and provider training.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 3 years ; Electronic: Retain in office 3 years

series_title: Money Follows the Person (MFP) Administration Files
series_description: Consumer case records for federally approved Money Follows the Person (MFP) program and Ohio Access Success Project. Includes consumer enrollment and intake, assessment and referral, cost cap for level of care, administrative approvals and maintenance documenting consumer medical history (diagnosis and treatment) and provider service to recipients.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 7 years or until all cases are closed

series_description: Consists of electronic records of participants in the Ohio Access Success Project. Records indicate participant name, identifiers, applicable dates, case disposition, and expended funds.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 60 days after record is closed and ; Electronic: Electronic data base: Retain in electronic format for 4 years until final payment has been made or until any audit findings or lawsuits have been resolved.

series_description: The PPR work flow will produce documents up to and including correspondence (emails, documentation of phone calls, provider-produced records, certified mail), overpayment reports, spreadsheets documenting audit work, internal quality control reviews, and other documents necessary to carry out this process.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until adjudication ; Electronic: Retain in office until adjudication

series_description: Documents relating to monitoring of alternative delivery systems. May include quality assurance reports, claims history reports, financial reports, historical data, capitation reports, reviews.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years in office, 5 years in Record Center ; Electronic: Retain 2 years in office, 5 years in Record Center

series_description: Manuals produced by ODM for internal personnel training.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced

series_description: Record contains all work documents used to write Ohio Health Plan Policy, such as policy research information referencing reasons for payment or non-payment of claims, samples of provider and/or patient claims, claims processing and reimbursement information, surveys of provider services, meeting notes, contractor reports, budget material, and related correspondence from constituents regarding the status of claims and services provided and any issues that have not been resolved.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office until policy is superseded, obsolete or replaced ; Paper: Retain in office until policy is superseded, obsolete or replaced

series_title: Ohio Administrative Code Rules and Medicaid Eligibility
series_description: Record contains policy letters, manual transmittal letters and action change letters; historical documentation which includes CRIS-E reports; memorandums and administrative decision support documents. File also includes public assistance policy and OAC rules of reference.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area 6 years and in records center until no longer of administrative value and audits are completed, audit reports released and all discrepancies are resolved. Review by State prior to disposition. ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced, the destroy.

series_description: Record supports existing inpatient/outpatient hospital payment system policy under the Medicaid Program. File contains, but is not limited to, printouts documenting payment policy rationale; financial statements; hospital cost data; third party data (Medicare supporting data); statistical analyses; and related reports that are the basis for existing hospital payments.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until claims are paid under the policy ; Electronic: Retain in office until the policy is superseded and audits are completed, reports are released and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: Record contains documentation supporting Ohio’s Hospital Care Assurance Program (HCAP) which compensates hospitals who provide a disproportionate share of care to indigent patients (Medicaid consumers, recipients below the poverty level, and those without health insurance). File includes, but is not limited to, itemized cost records supporting hospital claims; itemized description of services provided; patient medical and eligibility information; financial, medical reviews and claims validation audits to determine if hospitals are in compliance with policy.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until audits are completed, reports released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Paper: Retain in office 1 year after internal review is completed and 4 years in records center and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_title: Institution for Treatment of Medical Diseases/Disproportionate Share Hospital Payments
series_description: Record contains documentation supporting Ohio’s Institution for Treatment of Mental Diseases/Disproportionate Share Hospital Payments (IMD/DSH), which compensates hospitals who provide a disproportionate share of care to indigent patients (Medicaid consumers, recipients below the poverty level, and those without health insurance). File includes, but is not limited to, itemized cost records supporting hospital claims; itemized description of services provided; patient medical and eligibility information; financial, medical reviews and claims validation audits to determine if hospitals are in compliance with policy.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 1 year after internal review is completed and 4 years in records center and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Electronic: Retain until audits are completed, reports released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: Contracts with external vendors (e.g. Ohio Organ Transplant Organization, stem cell research organizations, and Permedion) which support hospital payment policy and services rendered under Medicaid. File contains, but is not limited to, contracts and bid proposals; meeting summaries; contract deliverables and supporting documentation, such as copies of purchase orders and invoices; and utilization reviews containing personal medical history and patient specific information that is used to determine medical necessity and quality of care of Medicaid recipients.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years after the end of the contract ; Paper: Retain in office 5 years after the end ofthe contract

series_description: Record supports statistical data regarding transportation services (Enhanced Medicaid Transportation: EMT) provided for consumers at the county level. The file contains, but is not limited to one-way trips, number of consumers and modes of transportation utilized documenting services provided and monies spent.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 1 year and in records center 4 years resolved. ; Electronic: Retain in office until policy is superseded

series_description: Interim/ final audit settlements of services provided at Community Alternative Funding System (CAFS), hospitals, passport program and public developmental centers. File contains but is not limited to cost report forms (alpha by provider name) and audit work papers and reports (numeric by provider number). May also contain the following forms: ODHS 2930, 2918, 2919, 2920, 2921, 2934, 2935, 2936, 2937, 2938, 2939, and 2220. All information pertaining to Hospital Franchise Fees will also be filled due to links cost reports.
retention_statement: Paper: In office and/ or in Storage Retain in office until adjudicated and in records center 5 years

series_description: Records include correspondence documenting requests for investigating an employee and/or situation within the agency. May include requests for outside agency (i.e., Highway Patrol, Auditor, OIG, etc.) assistance and/or referrals for investigations. Records also include any and all documentation regarding the investigation (i.e., request, referrals, case notes, interviews, preliminary investigations, etc.). (Most documents are originals; some are copies.)Investigations may also contain confidential ODJFS constituent records regarding public assistance, foster care, unemployment compensation, health care, etc. which is protected by law.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced ; Paper: Retain in office 2 years after completion of investigation or decision not to investigate. Then, transfer to the Records Center for 5 years

series_description: Operations manuals and/or documents which are part of the contingency plans for homeland security and safety, natural and man-made disasters that may occur within Ohio boundaries.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office until superseded, obsolete or replaced ; Paper: Retain in office until superseded, obsolete or replaced

series_description: Records include correspondence sent as notification to external customers regarding any decisions made by ODM concerning the customer''s threats against the agency or employee. May include notice of possible legal actions should violations of the notice occur.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for three (3) years of no further activity ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced

series_description: Requests for photo identification card for employees and/or contractors and the physical badge.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain electronic requests for three (3) years ; Paper: Retain paper requests for three (3) years

series_description: Records of visitors (customers, stakeholders, contractors, employees without their identification badges, etc.) signed into the suite.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office for five (5) years ; Paper: Retain in office for two (2) years

series_description: Records of access to/or in the ODM suite associated with a card holder''s (employee, contractor, etc.) authorized badge.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain records for two (2) years

series_description: Video camera footage of the lobby of the ODM suite wired to a digital video recorder (DVR). Footage retention varies, but routinely covers a 14 day period. Footage will not be reproduced after being recorded over on the DVR.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for the available period; typically 14 days.

series_description: Reports of unusual incidents occurring on ODM premises or while conducting business for ODM throughout the state (e.g. thefts, threats, injuries, data incidents, etc).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 7 years

series_description: Files contain active and inactive case documents, including pertinent supportive information (complaints and hearing records), and may include final disposition and informal complaint.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 60 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), indexed and quality control is complete, and ; Electronic: Retain 10 years

series_description: Records documenting inquiries and responses to civil rights/EEO issues.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years and ; Electronic: Retain in office 7 years and

series_description: Case files of employees coved under the Americans with Disabilities Act and Reasonable Accommodation guidelines. Records Include letters requesting reasonable accommodation with respect to parking, facilities, and equipment needed to perform their job, and personal medical information documenting their ADA qualification.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 7 years ; Paper: Retain in office 7 years

series_title: DODD Level One Wavier Review and Monitoring
series_description: Documentation to include documentation on approval, denials, termination, quality assurance reviews, compliance review reports, physical inspection reports, interview documentation (provider, enrollees, administrators).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years in office, 4 years in records center ; Electronic: Retain in office 3 years until receipt of final payment or until any audit findings or lawsuits have been resolved

series_title: Targeted Case Management (TCM) for DODD Compliance
series_description: Documentation to include reports on physical inspection of records and sites where services are provided: documented interviews with administrators, recipients and providers; service verification, medical necessity claims verification, all other records related to the administration/provision of TCM Services (Department of Development Disabilities).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years in office, 4 years in records center

series_description: The program provide Medicaid reimbursement for service provided to Medicaid eligible children ages 3 to 21 who receive services at school through an Individualized Education Plan. Allows reimbursement for cost for direct service provided by licensed individuals, administrative activities and transportation services. Records to include service utilization and fiscal reports, interagency agreements, program summaries, provide list, State Plan amendments.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years and destroy. ; Paper: Retain in office (1) year and

series_description: Files of Medicaid provider and consumer case records for federally approved ODJFS administered wavier, Success, and State Plan Home Care Programs. Record includes, but is not limited to, consumer enrollment and intake, service plans, assessment and referral, level of care, administrative approvals and maintenance documenting consumer medical history (diagnosis and treatment) and provider services to recipients. Records are processed and maintained at the Case Management Agency and shipped to the records center when the cases are closed and become inactive. Records are maintained electronically.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office at case management agency until case is closed and convert to electronic. Retain in office for 60 days after scanning. Destroy if material is released after the scanned material is reviewed. ; Microform: CD/DVD Retain in warehouse for 7 years and and released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_title: Private Duty Nursing (PDN) Authorization and Administration Files
series_description: Record contains, but not limited to, request for PDN Prior Authorization(form ODM 02374), assessments, acuity tool, prior authorizations, all supporting back up documentation, denial decisions, hearing notices and decisions, appeals summaries, PDN consumer case files, and reimbursement decisions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years in office, 4 years in records center

series_title: Ohio Home Care Enrollment and Waiting List Documentation
series_description: Documentation to include waiting lists, slot release spreadsheets, ODM 02399 Home and Community Based Service Waiver Application, and all written notifications.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office three (3) years and ; Electronic: Retain in office three (3) years until receipt of final payment or until any audit Findings or lawsuits have been resolved.

series_title: Managed Care Plan and Enhanced Care Management Plan Provider Agreements
series_description: Provider agreements between the State of Ohio and those administering managed care plans or enhanced care management plans to provide health care services at reduced cost to Medicaid eligible recipients.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 years after agreement expires and 5 years in the records center ; Electronic: Retain in office 5 years after agreement expires and 5 years in the records center

series_title: Ohio Home Care Waiver Contract Documentation
series_description: Contract records (i.e., home and community-based service programs and waivers), which include requests for Proposals and related documentation, and DAS contract documentation and amendments; quality management plans and reports; contract deliverable reports and responses; notices of operations deficiencies and corrective action plans; copies of invoices; contract expenditures and task tracking documents; meeting notes and related correspondence; Training Interpretation Memorandums(TIMS),presentation materials; waiver program training documentation including agendas, notes, handouts, evaluation, forms, program policies; interagency agreements; sub grants; and Memorandums of Understanding(MOU).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until agreement expires and in records center 15 years audits are completed, reports released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Electronic: Retain in office until no longer of administrative value, and

series_description: Rule, policy and program development files for the EPSDT (Early Periodic Screening Diagnosis & Treatment Prevention), program, which Reviews case specific request for unusual items that may be covered because of EPSDT. To include clinical files to include policy drafts, amendments, published rules, policy development documentation, proposed funding and grant applications, documented changes to the program, evaluations required by CMS (Centers for Medicare/Medicaid Services),and all other monitoring reports and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until program is closed ; Electronic: Retain in office for 5 years

series_title: Preadmission Screening and Resident Review (PASRR) Program Policy
series_description: Rule, policy and program development files for system reform effort, regarding the PASRR program, which prohibits nursing facilities from admitting/enrolling Individuals with Serious Mental Illness (SMI) and/or developmental disability (DD) without adequate evaluation are electronically stored. To include, policy drafts, amendments, published rules, policy development documentation, periodic program evaluation required by CMS. In addition, completed, PASRR evaluation and Resident Review Extension request and all other monitoring reports and related correspondence are securely stored on site.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years ; Electronic: Retain in office 7 years

series_description: Documents created to support the state plans relating to the Long-Term Services & Supports (LTSS) program to ensure distribution of allowable costs and adequate reimbursement to nursing homes and intermediate care facilities. Records document the development of long term care policies for client eligibility, services covered and system design. Files include, but are not limited to, analysis to determine the daily rate for services per recipient, how the state will reimburse the nursing homes and intermediate care facilities, statistical reports, cost trending, rule filing packages, contract and research documentation, and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 7 years or until state and federal audits are completed and released, and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_title: Level of Care (LOC) Program Policy and Development Files
series_description: Rule, policy and program development files for system reform effort, regarding Phase I Short-term and Phase II long-term care services. To include, policy drafts, amendments, published rules, policy development documentation, proposed funding and grant applications, documented changes to the program, periodic program evaluation required by CMS.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years ; Paper: Retain in office 1 years and

series_description: Record documenting the direct reimbursement to recipients, and assistance provided to obtain refunds from providers for out-of-pocket expenses for services rendered prior to receiving assistance from Medicaid. Files include voucher requests, provider billing information, copies of invoices, pharmacy receipts, credit card statements, power of attorney, and related correspondence that may contain confidential medical information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 7 years ; Paper: Retain in office 7 years

series_title: Ohio Home Care Waiver Program Policy and Development Files
series_description: Rule, policy and program development files for Ohio Home Care waiver program, which provides home and community-based alternatives to institutional long term care. To include, policy drafts, amendments, published rules, policy development documentation, proposed funding and grant applications, documented changes to the program, period program evaluations required by CMS, and all other monitoring reports and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 15 years ; Paper: Retain in office 10 years, and 5 years in record center

series_description: MITs (Medicaid Information Technology Systems)-Business Transformation documents including: Integration Government material; as is processed documentation, to include documentation, narratives and maps.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 2 years and 3 years in Record Center

series_description: The Provider Network Management system receives data from potential and current providers for enrollment, disenrollment, certification, revalidation, and credentialing purposes to be a Medicaid provider. This data contains information such as name, date of birth, social security number, driver''s license number, address and professional certification documents. The system also contains claims data and recipient eligibility and immunization records from other systems.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper copies of all project deliverables, artifacts, training materials and other non-system records, until scanned into machine readable format. Paper copies not scanned shall be kept for 7 years and ; Electronic: Three historical versions and one current version of machine readable formats of all project deliverables, artifacts, training materials and other non-system records should be maintained at any point of time, or 7 years, whichever comes first.

series_description: Record of grievance case files organized by case number and year for issue grievance, working-out-of-class, and disciplinary grievances. May contain original grievance forms, (ADM-4403 and 4405), and supporting documentation including, but not limited to information on Step 1 and Step 2 filling an responses, copies of classification specifications and position descriptions, grievance settlement or withdrawal information, arbitration decisions and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years or until arbitration is complete. ; Electronic: Retain in office 7 years or until arbitration is complete.

series_title: Long Term Care (LTC) Insurance Partnership Program Policy and Development Files
series_description: Rule, policy and program development files for the LTC Insurance Partnership program which is jointly administered by the Ohio Department of Insurance and Ohio Department of Medicaid. The program allows persons who purchase qualified long term care insurance policies from which claims are paid for long term care to be entitled to special resource exemptions used when the policy holders apply for Medicaid. The policy holders are allowed to keep resources up to the amount of claims the LTC insurance policy paid. Special estate recovery provisions also apply. The program was mandated by Ohio Law and records may include, policy drafts, amendments, published rules, policy development documentation, proposed funding and grant applications, documented changes to the program, Period program evaluations required by CMS, and all other monitoring reports and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office for 5 years Should be reviewed by State prior to disposition. ; Paper: Retain closed. Should be imaged

series_description: Hospital benefit documentation including records pertaining to HCAP, utilization reviews, quality reports, and payment system.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until obsolete or superseded, and ; Electronic: Retain in office until obsolete or superseded, and

series_description: Files contain but not limited to, monthly caseloads reports, monthly variances report, disbursement estimates, worksheets, budget projections and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: maintain budget reports and disbursement estimates for 5 years.

series_description: Record includes, but is not limited to, documents pertaining to, biennium budget expenditures.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain budget information through current biennium and major documents for 2 biennium thereafter.

series_description: A record of the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) adjustments that document the Agency’s funding expenditures and modifications to chart-fields. File includes, but is not limited to, a description of the adjustment and the appropriate coding needed for the adjustment. Information is electronically forwarded for entering into the Ohio Administrative Knowledge System (OAKS the State of Ohio’s accounting system).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain electronic version for at least 7 years after the grant liquidation period expires and

series_description: Record includes, but is not limited to, documents related to all federal cash draws in payment for Medicaid provider services and administrative expenditures (i.e., revenue documents, draw support documentation).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced;

series_title: Letter of Credit and Grant Revenue Reconciliation
series_description: Record includes federal grant documentation, grant requests and renewals, grant awards, and related correspondence. The records are used for budget references and audit settlements.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced

series_description: Record includes financial reports required by the federal government (Department of Health and Human Services)administering ODM federal programs. The supporting documentation contain expenditure statistical data by program compiled from ODM, CDJFS''s and sister state agencies. Files include backup documentation, worksheets and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced ; Paper: Retain 2 year in office and 8 years in records center. Send to State Archives for storage.

series_description: Schedule of financial reports: (OBM-GAAP - Generally Accepted Accounting Principles, used to compile the Ohio Comprehensive Annual Financial Report. SWCAP - ODM Allocation of the statewide Cost Allocation Plan. Schedule of Federal Financial Assistance - schedule of federal grants dollars received by ODM during the state fiscal year.) Files include backup documentation, worksheets, and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced ; Paper: Retain in Medicaid records center, provided audits have been completed and released, and all discrepancies have been resolved.

series_title: Long Term Care Provider Agreement Files
series_description: Long Term Care facility provider agreements for Medicaid Facilities. Files contain documentation used to support payment/non-payment of services and nursing facilities (NF). Files contain documentation used to support payment/non-payment of services, reflecting changes in facility operations and showing how the agency fulfills its contracts with the federal government to administer the Medicaid program. Includes licenses, cost reports, rate settling reconsiderations and adjustments, adjudication master file, compliance audit findings, pending litigation, complaints about the facility or its patients, facility certification sheets, certification survey packets, operator bankruptcies, exit conferences and facility evaluations, and related correspondence through 2015.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until agreement is canceled, and

series_title: Ohio Medicaid Consumer Hotline Screen Navigation
series_description: All records of recordings of the customer service agent navigation through the Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system for telephone calls received by the Ohio Medicaid Consumer Hotline identified as being needed by the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 90 days

series_description: The Systems Integrator (SI) records document the mechanics (techniques and technology) for Ohio Medicaid Enterprise System (OMES) business modules to communicate data securely by receiving and transmitting transactions. The SI records shall include, but are not limited to, User Acceptance Testing materials, training materials, and other records created to support SI module.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper copies of all project deliverables, artifacts, training materials and other non-system records, until scanned into machine readable format. Paper copies not scanned shall be kept for 7 years and ; Electronic: Three historical versions and one current version of machine readable formats of all project deliverables, artifacts, training materials and other non-system records should be maintained at any point of time, or 7 years, whichever comes first.

series_description: The Systems Integrator (SI) data is the data generated or recorded as part of the mechanism (techniques and technology) used by Ohio Medicaid Enterprise Systems (OMES) business modules to communicate data securely by receiving and transmitting transactions. The SI system data shall include, but is not limited to, system log information, and any other system information generated or recorded to support SI module.
retention_statement: Electronic: Transfer transactional data from all modules, and any other system records, to EDW. Retain system logs in SI for 13 months from the date the information was logged

series_description: All electronic and paper records associated with Single Pharmacy Benefit Manager (SPBM) Contractor administration of pharmacy benefits, including claims, prior authorizations, eligibility, customer service records, clinical data and data received from managed care organizations
retention_statement: Paper: 10 years ; Electronic: 10 years

series_description: All electronic and paper records associated with Single Pharmacy Benefit Manager (SPBM) Contractor administration of pharmacy benefits, including claims, prior authorizations, eligibility, customer service records, clinical data and data received from managed care organizations
retention_statement: Paper: Ten (10) years ; Electronic: Ten (10) years

series_description: The Provider Network Management (PNM) module will store and process data associated with provider management, screening, credentialing, and enrollment. Project records will be available in paper and machine readable formats.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper copies of all project deliverables, artifacts, training materials and other non-system records until scanned into a machine readable format. Paper copies not scanned shall be kept for 7 years and ; Electronic: Three historical versions and one current version of machine readable formats of all project deliverables, artifacts, training materials, and other non-system records should be maintained at any point of time, or 7 years after the contract terminates.

series_description: The Provider Network Management (PNM) module will store and process data associated with provider management, screening, credentialing, and enrollment.
retention_statement: Electronic: Transactional data associated with the provisioning and management of providers as well as other provider related system data should be retained for 10 years beyond provider agreement termination.

series_title: Fiscal Intermediary Records and Data Retention Schedule
series_description: The Fiscal Intermediary (FI) module will store and process data associated with adjudication and payment of Fee-for-Service (FFS) claims, routing of Managed Care claims submitted by Managed Care participating Providers to Managed Care Organizations (MCOs), processing and management of FFS Prior Authorization requests and responses, routing of Managed Care Prior Authorization requests and responses between Managed Care participating Providers and MCOs, receiving Managed Care Encounters from MCOs, and reconciliation activities. The FI vendor will also be responsible for storing and processing data resulting from ODM’s financial management operations.
retention_statement: Paper: Ten (10) years ; Electronic: Ten (10) years

series_description: The records contain information pertaining to audits conducted by outside auditing entities (CMS, HHS-OIG, OBM, AOS, etc.) as part of the agency's audit coordination and remediation efforts. Records may include systems infrastructure, PHI/PII/CPI, process narratives, and other responses to audit requests from any auditing entity. At the closure of an audit, the auditing entity sends ODM a report with remediation recommendations. ODM develops and completes a corrective action plan and documents the actions taken.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 4 years and 6 years in storage, (10 years total) ; Electronic: Retain on agency drive for 10 years
